channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Next Week Is Going to Be Pure Chaos!!
item.description: Next week my life will become a distributed system under heavy load. Why?? Because three things are happening at the same time: College midsems starts Monday GSOC proposal writing Trying to push...

item.title: DevOps From Scratch: Entry #05
item.description: Chapters 6 and 7 of the Linux Foundation LFS101 course cover System Configuration from the Graphical Interface and Common Applications. On the surface, these sound like the "not-so-technical"...

item.title: Apprenez à forcer l'API de l'IA à vous répondre exclusivement dans un format JSON strict et validé
item.description: Structured Output : Forcez l'IA à parler JSON Si vous avez déjà essayé d'intégrer une réponse d'IA directement dans une application classique (Web ou Mobile), vous avez obligatoirement rencontré...

item.title: I Checked What Security Vulnerabilities AI Coding Tools Actually Introduce
item.description: Last month I started going through PRs and open-source repos, cataloging the security vulnerabilities that AI coding tools actually introduce. Not theoretical risks. Actual patterns showing up in...

item.title: How to Trust-Gate Your AI Agent API in 3 Lines of Code
item.description: In January 2026, an AI agent called Lobstar Wilde lost $250,000 in a single transaction. Nobody had checked its reputation before giving it access. That's the problem with the current agent...

item.title: We Escaped Tutorial Hell, Only to Enter "Prompt Hell"
item.description: It’s a story I see every week now. Let’s call him "Dev." Dev is a junior engineer in 2026. He has a stunning portfolio. In just six months, he built a SaaS boilerplate, a fitness tracker, and a...

item.title: The Great AI Agent Consolidation Has Begun
item.description: If you've been building with AI agents for the past year, you've felt the chaos. Every month, a new framework. Every week, a new "standard." Pick LangChain? CrewAI ships something interesting. Bet...

item.title: We Built a VS Code Extension That Triple-Checks AI-Generated Code for Security Vulnerabilities
item.description: Studies show roughly 40% of AI-generated code contains at least one exploitable vulnerability. We accept Copilot suggestions with a quick Tab press and move on. But who's checking the code your AI...

item.title: OAuth2 vs OpenID Connect: What's the Difference?
item.description: OAuth2 and OpenID Connect are often mentioned together, and many developers assume they solve the same problem. In reality, they serve different purposes. Understanding the difference is essential...

item.title: "bash-guard: Stop Claude Code From Running Dangerous Commands"
item.description: Claude Code can run any bash command on your machine. That's what makes it powerful. It's also what makes it dangerous. Most of the time, it runs sensible commands. But sometimes, through...

item.title: Vizio accounts are becoming Walmart accounts
item.description: Walmart bought Vizio in 2024, and now it's taking the next steps to formally fold the TV maker into itself by phasing out Vizio accounts. Now, when you purchase a new Vizio TV, you'll be asked to...

item.title: The OpenClaw superfan meetup serves optimism and lobster
item.description: The woman at the door wore a plush lobster headdress. She sat in the front hallway of a multistory event venue in Manhattan, beside a bundle of wristbands. If she granted you one, the world of...

item.title: A bite-sized adventure that puts a wrench into the classic Zelda formula
item.description: There are a lot of games that try to emulate The Legend of Zelda, but few that manage to capture that spirit in such a small, concise package as Ratcheteer DX. The postapocalyptic game only takes...

item.title: Apple’s cheap laptop looks like a winner
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 118, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, please take my Switch away so I can get some work done, and also...

item.title: The Corvette ZR1X hybrid can outpace million-dollar sports cars for a fraction of the cost
item.description: When Elon Musk burst onto the scene in his little Tesla Roadster, it seemed a matter of time before electricity rendered gas-powered sports cars obsolete. It hasn't worked out that way. Automakers...

item.title: DJI will pay $30K to the man who accidentally hacked 7,000 Romo robovacs
item.description: On Valentine's Day, I brought you a story that's since made headlines all around the world: How one man, just trying to steer his DJI robot vacuum with a PlayStation gamepad, discovered an entire...

item.title: This Jammer Wants to Block Always-Listening AI Wearables. It Probably Won’t Work
item.description: Deveillance’s Spectre I, developed by a recent Harvard grad, wants to give people control over the always-on wearables surrounding their lives. The problem? Physics.

item.title: Valve says it still plans to ship the Steam Machine in 2026
item.description: A blog post from Valve on Friday initially seemed to throw cold water on the idea that the Steam Machine, Steam Frame, and Steam Controller would arrive in 2026 at all. But Valve tells The Verge...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid in Byzantine Monk Cooking
item.description: This is a very weird story about how squid stayed on the menu of Byzantine monks by falling between the cracks of dietary rules. At Constantinople’s Monastery of Stoudios, the kitchen didn’t...

item.title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
item.description: Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried...

item.title: The best microSD Express card for the Switch 2 is the cheapest one you can find
item.description: Unlike the original Switch, the Nintendo Switch 2 requires microSD Express cards for storing and playing games. While physically identical to microSD cards (aside from a small, easy-to-miss “EX”...

item.title: The Trump administration says it can’t process tariff refunds because of computer problems
item.description: The US Customs and Border Protection says it currently can't comply with an order to process billions of dollars in refunds stemming from tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. In a filing on...

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

item.title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
item.description: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
item.description: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous...

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon, the SaaSpocalypse, and why competitions is good, actually
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous...

item.title: How our open-source AI model SpeciesNet is helping to promote wildlife conservation
item.description: Photos of animals being identified by the SpeciesNet AI model

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.description: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is in and Anthropic is out as a supplier of AI technology for the US defense department. This news caps a week of bluster by the highest officials in the US government towards some of the...
